🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Market sentiment is dominated by major regulatory shifts as the Trump administration officially kills the U.S. CBDC. While the FBI executes a record $8B seizure, institutional friction grows over the landmark Clarity Act's stablecoin provisions. 🚨FBI Executes Record $8 Billion Crypto Seizure in "Operation Blackout" The FBI has dismantled a global fraud network, seizing over 127,000 Bitcoin worth approximately $8 billion from Prince Holding Group CEO Chen Zhi. This marks the largest crypto forfeiture in U.S. history, recovering funds tied to Southeast Asian trafficking and scam compounds. Read full story 🏛️Trump Administration Officially Rejects U.S. Central Bank Digital Currency President Trump has fulfilled a campaign promise by formally killing plans for a U.S. CBDC, citing concerns over government surveillance and financial privacy. The move cements a policy shift toward private stablecoin infrastructure rather than a state-controlled digital dollar. See details 🏦JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon Vows to Fight "Clarity Act" Over Stablecoin Interest JPMorgan’s Jamie Dimon has launched a fierce opposition to the Clarity Act, specifically targeting provisions that allow crypto firms to pay interest on stablecoin deposits. Banks argue the bill creates unfair regulatory disparities by bypassing traditional reserve requirements. Read full story 🌐Cardano Foundation Cancels 2026 Global Summit Following Treasury Vote The Cardano Foundation has officially canceled its upcoming 2026 summit after a community governance vote for treasury funding fell just short of the required threshold. The decision highlights the growing pains of decentralized governance in managing large-scale ecosystem events. See details ⚖️SEC Sues Privvy Founder Over $12.3 Million AI-Driven Crypto Scheme The SEC has filed a lawsuit against the founder of Privvy, alleging an investment scheme where "AI trading bots" were found to be non-existent. The regulator claims the project defrauded investors of $12.3 million by leveraging artificial intelligence hype to mask a traditional Ponzi structure. Read full story 🏗️Ethereum "Glamsterdam" Hard Fork Set to Triple Network Speed in 2026 Ethereum developers have confirmed the "Glamsterdam" upgrade roadmap, aiming to increase Layer 1 speeds to 10,000 transactions per second. This hard fork is

73853.subject: 🟪 Formalizing disclosure in token markets
73853.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/666ae944-5cae-4c6c-abd6-970020388daf/BD-Banner-Sponsor_Template.png?t=1780094904)
Follow image link: (https://peaq.xyz)
Caption: 

# Formalizing Disclosure in Token Markets

Token markets have an unsolved information asymmetry problem.

When you buy a stock, you get a prospectus, a 10-K, and quarterly filings with audited numbers, disclosed insiders, and legally binding representations about material information. The disclosure burden may be imperfect, but the framework is legible. When purchasing a token, the burden of collecting this information from various governance forums, Discord channels, X/Twitter accounts, and blog posts falls on the holder. Market maker agreements, if they exist, are buried or undisclosed. This gap between what issuers know and what tokenholders can access is structural, and it has been the engine behind most of crypto's worst moments of the last decade.

Today, Blockworks launches the [Transparency Alliance](https://blockworks.com/insights/blockworks-launches-transparency-alliance) to address this directly.

The Alliance formalizes the [Token Transparency Framework](https://blockworks.com/token-transparency) (TTF), which Blockworks first introduced in June 2025, into an industry-wide standard backed by the actual participants who control token capital markets. Founding members span exchanges (Coinbase, Kraken, Binance.US), custodians (Anchorage Digital, BitGo, Copper), market makers (GSR, FalconX, Auros), funds (Grayscale, VanEck, Bitwise), and protocols (Jito, Aerodrome, Aave). The logic follows that a framework only becomes a standard when the institutions controlling listings, diligence, and capital deployment are committed to enforcing it.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/47b3b419-e4ef-41b5-8a26-5fed8f640f6c/image.png?t=1780170957)
Caption: 

The TTF itself is organized into two filings. The B-1 serves as a baseline, functioning as the S-1 equivalent for digital assets. The B-2 is a continuously updated disclosure for mature protocols. Both are classification-agnostic, which removes the legal friction that has historically made founders reluctant to put anything in writing. Filings receive labels reflecting how much of the disclosure template has been filled in, not a qualitative grade. A "Partial" label is a legible signal to exchanges and tokenholders about what information remains unavailable. 

With Congress [advancing](https://blockworks.com/newsletter/0xresearch/issue/post_10a80b5c-54d0-4f19-819f-fb29d10d6bd7) the CLARITY Act, formal regulatory frameworks for token classification are moving to a near-term reality. Industry-led disclosure standards that precede regulation tend to shape it. The pattern has repeated itself across financial history, and in each case the volun

73274.subject: Svadobná výzdoba
73274.body:
Zdravíme nevestu💍🤍,

Ďakujeme veľmi pekne za vašu správu.. Termín svadby máme ešte voľný a veľmi
radi budeme súčasťou vašej svadby.🤍

Spravidla sa s klientami stretávame osobne v showroome v Rozhanovciach
(kúsok od Košíc) kde si spolu vieme vyskladať svadobnú výzdobu a na základe
toho vytvoriť cenovú ponuku na mieru pre vás ak by ste mali záujem prosím
navrhnite deň a čas kedy by vám to najviac vyhovovalo aby sa časovo radi
prispôsobíme vášmu času. Poprípade, ak sme mimo Košíc či Prešova, radi si s
Vami dohodneme videohovor. Prosím navrhnite deň a čas, ktorý by Vám
vyhovoval najviac, pokojne aj cez víkend aj vo večerných hodinách. 🙂
Najbližsie voľne termíny máme ešte aj tento týždeň. Forme stretnutia sa
radi prispôsobíme Vám, čo je pre Vás praktickejšie ☺️

Budeme sa tešiť na prípadnú spoluprácu s Vami.

S pozdravom

Lenka Gattiker,
Vaša osobná posh asistentka

73231.subject: From a Cornell Lab to BlackRock. The Avalanche Story. | One Block Weekly #11
73231.body:
Hello,

Welcome back to One Block Weekly. One coin, one story.

While most blockchains were still arguing about speed, one quietly went and built a different kind of network altogether.

🏔️ Avalanche (AVAX)

Emin Gun Sirer is a Cornell University computer science professor who had been researching distributed systems long before most people knew what a blockchain was.

In 2018, his team published a whitepaper introducing a new consensus mechanism, a way for a network to agree on what is true, that was faster and more scalable than anything Bitcoin or Ethereum used. They called it Avalanche.

Ava Labs was founded to build a blockchain around it. Avalanche launched its mainnet in September 2020, raised $230 million in a public token sale in 48 hours, and immediately positioned itself as the platform serious institutions would eventually choose.

The pitch was simple: all the programmability of Ethereum, none of the congestion.

⚡ A Few Moments That Shaped Avalanche

2021 - AVAX surged over 2,700% in a single year, hitting an all-time high of around $147. Developers flooded in, and Avalanche briefly became the fourth largest blockchain by total value locked.

2022 - Avalanche partnered with Deloitte to build a disaster relief reimbursement platform for US government agencies. A blockchain landing a Big Four accounting firm and a government contract in the same deal was a first.

2023 - The subnet model gained traction. Major institutions, including financial firms and gaming companies, began launching their own custom blockchains, called subnets, built on top of Avalanche infrastructure.

2024 - BlackRock and other asset managers began tokenising real-world assets on Avalanche, bringing government bonds and funds onto the blockchain. Institutional adoption had moved from talk to action.

🔢 What Makes It Different

Avalanche finalises transactions in under two seconds, faster than almost any major blockchain. But the more interesting feature is subnets: custom blockchains that can be spun up within the Avalanche ecosystem, each with its own rules, validators, and token economics.

For institutions that want the benefits of blockchain without sharing space with memecoins and DeFi protocols, that is a compelling offer.

🤯 Three Weird Avalanche Facts

The founder once publicly exposed a flaw in a rival blockchain. Emin Gun Sirer identified and disclosed a critical vulnerability in Bitcoin's network years before Avalanche existed. He is one of the few people in crypto who arrived with an academic reputation already intact.

A major gaming subnet launched and went quiet. One of the highest profile early subnets was built for a blockchain gaming ecosystem. It launched with significant fanfare and struggled to find its audience, becoming an early lesson in the gap between institutional infrastructure and consumer adoption.

The whitepaper was published anonymously. The original Avalanche consensus paper was released und
